title: vexibia pachycarpa
intro: Vexibia pachycarpa, also known as the Bael tree, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of Asia and is a member of the family Rutaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Vexibia pachycarpa include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and prevent the growth of certain types of cancer cells. In herbal preparations, the bark, leaves, and fruit of the tree are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
